DirectMusic Content Analyzer (AudAnalyze)

The Microsoft® DirectMusic® content analyzer tool (AudAnalyze) examines your title's DirectMusic content and writes a factory function containing a list of DirectMusic objects needed to run your content. When objects are omitted from this list, their code is also omitted from your application by the linker, decreasing your title's size. If you use the DirectMusic default factory function, it must include code for all possible DirectMusic objects since it doesn't have any information about the content your title plans to use.

Based on a content list that you provide, AudAnalyze creates a file containing a function named MyFactory that replaces the DirectMusicDefaultFactory function. The MyFactory function contains a list of the DirectMusic objects needed to play your content. If your code passes MyFactory instead of DirectMusicDefaultFactory to DirectMusicInitializeEx, the linker will exclude unneeded DirectMusic objects from your title, decreasing its overall size. (DirectMusicDefaultFactory includes all the DirectMusic objects, whether they are required or not.)

Note    The AudAnalyze tool cannot be entirely accurate for all content. Some required objects may be omitted from MyFactory. If this is the case, when DirectMusic tries to create an object of that type, MyFactory will print an error message stating the CLSID of the unknown object, and the function will then call DebugBreak.

  • Usage
  • Requirements
  • Examples
  • Object Types
  • Converting Existing DirectMusic Applications

Usage

audanalyze [SEGMENT | LIST]... [[+|-]ObjectType][...] [/All] [/Q] OutputFile
Option Description
SEGMENT The name of a DirectMusic segment file, or a wildcard specifying a set of files.
LIST A file ending in .txt that contains a list of segments, one per line. May not contain wildcards. Relative path names in list files are relative to the current directory, which is not necessarily the list file's directory.
ObjectType A DirectMusic object (see list below).
OutputFile Output file containing MyFactory.
+ Forces inclusion of an object type.
- Forces exclusion of an object type.
/all Forces inclusion of all object types.
/Q Quiet mode. Does not ask before overwriting destination file.

Requirements

AudAnalyze requires that Microsoft® DirectX® 8.0 or higher be installed on the system.

Examples

This section contains examples demonstrating how to use the AudAnalyze tool.

Simply analyze one segment:

audanalyze MySegment.sgt Factory.cpp

Analyze two segments:

audanalyze MySegment.sgt AnotherSegment.sgt Factory.cpp

Analyze all segments in these subdirectories:

audanalyze Disco\*.sgt HipHop\*.sgt Factory.cpp

Analyze all segments in this list:

audanalyze MyPlayList.txt Factory.cpp

Include everything:

audanalyze /all Factory.cpp

Object Types

The following table contains the list of object types with their corresponding parameter name and GUID.

Object Type (CLSID) Corresponding Parameter Name GUID
CLSID_DirectMusicCollection DMCollection 480ff4b0-28b2-11d1-bef700c04fbf8fef
CLSID_DirectMusicSynth DMSynth 58c2b4d0-46e7-11d1-89ac00a0c9054129
CLSID_DirectMusicFileStream DMFileStream f12f2c7d-3651-486f-b9fa16e11d1524fd
CLSID_DirectMusicStreamStream DMStreamStream f34feac1-e3af-49ad-83970bed323ef96b
CLSID_DirectMusicMemStream DMMemStream 75ccb447-8d3f-4154-abad5960aed4ba63
CLSID_DirectMusicSegment DMSegment d2ac2882-b39b-11d1-870400600893b1bd
CLSID_DirectMusicLoader DMLoader d2ac2892-b39b-11d1-870400600893b1bd
CLSID_DirectMusicTempoTrack DMTempoTrack d2ac2885-b39b-11d1-870400600893b1bd
CLSID_DirectMusicTimeSigTrack DMTimeSigTrack d2ac2888-b39b-11d1-870400600893b1bd
CLSID_DirectMusicBandTrack DMBandTrack d2ac2894-b39b-11d1-870400600893b1bd
CLSID_DirectMusicPerformance DMPerformance d2ac2881-b39b-11d1-870400600893b1bd
CLSID_DirectMusicBand DMBand 79ba9e00-b6ee-11d1-86be00c04fbf8fef
CLSID_DirectMusicSeqTrack DMSeqTrack d2ac2886-b39b-11d1-870400600893b1bd
CLSID_DirectSoundWave DSWave 8a667154-f9cb-11d2-ad8a0060b0575abc
CLSID_DirectMusicWaveTrack DMWaveTrack eed36461-9ea5-11d3-9bd10080c7150a74
CLSID_DirectMusicChordTrack DMChordTrack d2ac288b-b39b-11d1-870400600893b1bd
CLSID_DirectMusicCommandTrack DMCommandTrack d2ac288c-b39b-11d1-870400600893b1bd
CLSID_DirectMusicStyleTrack DMStyleTrack d2ac288d-b39b-11d1-870400600893b1bd
CLSID_DirectMusicPatternTrack DMPatternTrack d2ac2897-b39b-11d1-870400600893b1bd
CLSID_DirectMusicMotifTrack DMMotifTrack d2ac288e-b39b-11d1-870400600893b1bd
CLSID_DirectMusicStyle DMStyle d2ac288a-b39b-11d1-870400600893b1bd
CLSID_DirectMusicMelodyFormulationTrack DMMelodyFormulationTrack b0684266-b57f-11d2-97f900c04fa36e58
CLSID_DirectMusicComposer DMComposer d2ac2890-b39b-11d1-870400600893b1bd
CLSID_DirectMusicChordMap DMChordMap d2ac288f-b39b-11d1-870400600893b1bd
CLSID_DirectMusicChordMapTrack DMChordMapTrack d2ac2896-b39b-11d1-870400600893b1bd
CLSID_DirectMusicSignPostTrack DMSignPostTrack f17e8672-c3b4-11d1-870b00600893b1bd
CLSID_AutDirectMusicAudioPath ADMAudioPath 2c5f9b72-7148-4d97-bfc968a0e076bebd
CLSID_AutDirectMusicAudioPathConfig ADMAudioPathConfig 1cebde3e-6b91-484a-af485e4f4ed6b1e1
CLSID_AutDirectMusicSong ADMSong a16f1761-b6d8-42eb-8d574a44fedd3bd2
CLSID_AutDirectMusicPerformance ADMPerformance a861c6e2-fcfc-11d2-8bc900600893b1b6
CLSID_AutDirectMusicSegment ADMSegment 4062c116-0270-11d3-8bcb00600893b1b6
CLSID_DirectMusicScript DMScript 810b5013-e88d-11d2-8bc100600893b1b6
CLSID_DirectMusicScriptTrack DMScriptTrack 4108fa85-3586-11d3-8bd700600893b1b6
CLSID_DirectMusicContainer DMContainer 9301e380-1f22-11d3-8226d2fa76255d47
CLSID_AutDirectMusicSegmentState ADMSegmentState ebf2320a-2502-11d3-8bd100600893b1b6
CLSID_DirectMusicMuteTrack DMMuteTrack d2ac2898-b39b-11d1-870400600893b1bd
CLSID_DirectMusicMarkerTrack DMMarkerTrack 55a8fd00-4288-11d3-9bd18a0d61c88835
CLSID_DirectMusicSong DMSong aed5f0a5-d972-483d-a384649dfeb9c181
CLSID_DirectMusicAudioPathConfig DMAudioPathConfig ee0b9ca0-a81e-11d3-9bd10080c7150a74
CLSID_DirectMusicGraph DMGraph d2ac2884-b39b-11d1-870400600893b1bd
CLSID_DirectMusicSysExTrack DMSysExTrack d2ac2887-b39b-11d1-870400600893b1bd
CLSID_DirectMusicSegmentTriggerTrack DMSegmentTriggerTrack bae4d665-4ea1-11d3-8bda00600893b1b6
CLSID_DirectMusicLyricsTrack DMLyricsTrack 995c1cf5-54ff-11d3-8bda00600893b1b6
CLSID_DirectMusicParamControlTrack DMParamControlTrack 4be0537b-5c19-11d3-8bdc00600893b1b6

Unless you override them, the following objects are included by default, regardless of content:

  • CLSID_DirectMusicFileStream
  • CLSID_DirectMusicStreamStream
  • CLSID_DirectMusicMemStream
  • CLSID_DirectMusicComposer
  • CLSID_DirectMusicChordTrack
  • CLSID_DirectMusicChordMapTrack
  • CLSID_DirectMusicCommandTrack

If you are using scripts, the following object types are added automatically. If your scripts don't use the related object, you may be able to omit it. For example if your scripts don't use segments, try omitting CLSID_AutDirectMusicSegment.

  • CLSID_AutDirectMusicAudioPath
  • CLSID_AutDirectMusicPerformance
  • CLSID_AutDirectMusicSegmentState
  • CLSID_AutDirectMusicSong
  • CLSID_AutDirectMusicSegment
  • CLSID_AutDirectMusicAudioPathConfig

Transitions (via scripts or IDirectMusicPerformance8::PlaySegmentEx) require the following objects. (If you are not using transitions and are not explicitly using these objects in your content, you may be able to omit them.

  • CLSID_DirectMusicComposer
  • CLSID_DirectMusicChordTrack
  • CLSID_DirectMusicChordMapTrack
  • CLSID_DirectMusicCommandTrack

Converting Existing DirectMusic Applications

To convert existing DirectMusic applications to take advantage of the DirectMusic content analyzer, complete the following steps:

  1. Use AudAnalyze to analyze all of your content by either putting all your content in one directory, or by creating a file list and then running the tool.
  2. Include the file generated by AudAnalyze in one of your existing source files. Make sure that the source file also includes DMusicI.h.
  3. #include "factory.cpp"
  4. Replace your call to DirectMusicInitialize with DirectMusicInitializeEx or DirectMusicInitializeFixedSizeHeaps (passing it the MyFactory function).

    Note    Currently, there is no convenient way to keep your heap behavior while using this feature.

  5. Compile and run your application. If it runs correctly, go directly to step 9.
  6. If your application asserted, note the GUID it was looking for.
  7. Use the table above to find the corresponding parameter.
  8. Run AudAnalyze again on your content, adding the parameter preceded by a "+" (for example, for CLSID_DirectMusicLoader, add +DMLoader).
  9. Go back to step 4.
  10. Your application should be running at this point. However, you may have extra objects in your MyFactory function that aren't needed. If you see an object like CLSID_DirectMusicChordMapTrack used in MyFactory, but your content contains no chordmap tracks, it's a good candidate for removal. Try commenting it out, or rerun AudAnalyze with the -DMChordMapTrack parameter. If you get no asserts, you've just decreased your code size!!
